problem_text: Show that the Ratio Test is inconclusive for sum_{n=1}^infinity (n + 1)/(n^3 + 2), then determine convergence by another method.

content.meaning: L = lim |a_(n+1)/a_n|; if L < 1 the series converges, if L > 1 it diverges, and if L = 1 the test is inconclusive.

content.label: form a_(n+1)/a_n = [(n+2)/((n+1)^3 + 2)] * [(n^3 + 2)/(n+1)]
content.purpose: build the ratio of consecutive terms

content.label: Ratio Test inconclusive (L=1)
content.applies_when: L = 1 because numerator and denominator are both cubic in n after expanding; L = 1 does NOT imply divergence, it means the test decides nothing.

content.label: since L = 1, the Ratio Test is inconclusive, so switch to another method rather than concluding divergence
content.action: since L = 1, the Ratio Test is inconclusive, so switch to another method rather than concluding divergence
content.purpose: recognize the Ratio Test cannot decide this series

content.meaning: since a_n ~ n/n^3 = 1/n^2, compare with b_n = 1/n^2: if lim a_n/b_n is finite and positive, they share convergence behavior.

content.label: compute lim [(n+1)/(n^3+2)]/[1/n^2] = 1, finite and positive; since sum 1/n^2 converges (p=2), sum (n+1)/(n^3+2) converges
content.action: compute lim [(n+1)/(n^3+2)]/[1/n^2] = 1, finite and positive; since sum 1/n^2 converges (p=2), sum (n+1)/(n^3+2) converges
content.purpose: resolve convergence with the Limit Comparison Test
